title: GitHub - gnosislabstech/marrow: Marrow: self-hosted semantic + lexical search over your AI coding sessions and notes. Every answer cites its source; your corpus never leaves your infrastructure.
excerpt: Marrow: self-hosted semantic + lexical search over your AI coding sessions and notes. Every answer cites its source; your corpus never leaves your infrastructure. - gnosislabstech/marrow
content_html:
Self-hosted semantic + lexical search over your AI coding sessions and notes. Every answer cites its source, and your corpus never leaves infrastructure you control.

You accumulate thousands of Claude Code sessions — decisions, debugging arcs, the why behind the code. This engine ingests that corpus into your own database and lets you ask it questions, with every result traced back to a real turn in a real session. No phone-home, no shared backend, no answer without a citation.

The product name is configurable (CB_PRODUCT_NAME) and not load-bearing — nothing in the code branches on it.

Try it in 60 seconds (zero key)

A disposable local stack (pgvector + PostgREST) seeded with a small synthetic corpus, exercising the real retrieval path with no API key:

npm install
npm run demo:up      # pgvector + PostgREST
npm run demo:seed    # schema + synthetic corpus
npm run demo         # cited results
npm run demo:down

See quickstart/README.md for the full walkthrough, including the one-time keyed step that enables the semantic (match-by-meaning) demo.

How it works

Stage What happens
Ingest Stream-parse session transcripts → turn windows → privacy pre-scan → embed
Store Postgres + pgvector (halfvec(1024)), RLS deny-by-default
Retrieve Hybrid: pgvector cosine + tsvector lexical, fused by Reciprocal Rank Fusion
Rerank Optional cross-encoder rerank of the top candidates
Answer Synthesized response with [N] citations to the source chunks

Embeddings come from Voyage (default voyage-4, 1024-dim). Retrieval-prefix generation (Contextual Retrieval) can use Anthropic or DeepSeek. All providers use your API keys; see SECURITY.md.

What it indexes

  • Claude Code sessions — the standard ~/.claude/projects tree (the default source).
  • Optional exports — Claude.ai, ChatGPT, and Telegram conversation exports, and a generic markdown "memory" directory. Configure these in src/sources.config.ts (copy src/sources.config.ts.example).

Bring your own corpus

  1. Provision Postgres with pgvector ≥ 0.7 (a Supabase project works out of the box, or any PostgREST + pgvector).

  2. Apply the migrations in supabase/migrations/.

  3. Copy .env.example → .env and fill in your database + Voyage credentials.

  4. (Optional) copy src/sources.config.ts.example → src/sources.config.ts to point at your own corpus.

  5. Ingest:

    npm run bootstrap:dry   # count + cost estimate, no writes
npm run bootstrap       # live ingest

Then search from the CLI (cb search / cb answer) or wire the MCP server into your editor.

Configuration

Everything owner-, brand-, or model-specific lives in src/config.ts with neutral defaults, overridable via environment variables:

Env Default Purpose
CB_PRODUCT_NAME Marrow Display name in banners / prompts
CB_CLI_NAME cb CLI name in help text
EMBEDDING_MODEL voyage-4 Embedding model
RERANKER_MODEL rerank-2.5-lite Reranker model
DEFAULT_OWNER owner Owner key scoping rows

Secret keys (database, Voyage, etc.) come from your environment — see .env.example.

What it deliberately omits

This is the engine: ingest, retrieve, cite. It does not profile you, build recommendations, or layer analytics on top of your corpus. Those are out of scope here by design. The engine's job is faithful, cited recall — nothing more.

Develop

npm test          # node:test suite
npm run typecheck # tsc --noEmit

Contributions welcome by discussion — see CONTRIBUTING.md. Licensed under Apache 2.0.

Marrow is the open-core release of the retrieval engine Gnosis Labs runs in production. The analytics and insight layers above it stay closed; the engine — ingest, retrieve, cite — is all here. Maintainer contact: cap@gnosislabs.tech (security reports: see SECURITY.md).

sentences.zh: 其之上的分析和洞察层保持关闭状态；引擎——摄取、检索、引用——都在这里。

content_html_zh:
对您的 AI 编码会话和笔记进行自托管语义 + 词汇搜索。 每个答案都会引用其来源，并且您的语料库永远不会离开您控制的基础设施。

您积累了数千个 Claude Code 会话——决策、调试弧线、代码背后的原因。 该引擎将该语料库提取到您自己的数据库中，并让您向它提问，每个结果都可以追溯到真实会话中的真实回合。 没有回拨电话，没有共享后端，没有引用就没有答案。

产品名称是可配置的（CB_PRODUCT_NAME）并且不承载——代码中没有任何分支。

60 秒内尝试一下（零键）

一次性本地堆栈（pgvector + PostgREST）以小型合成语料库为种子，在没有 API 密钥的情况下执行真实的检索路径：

npm install
npm run demo:up      # pgvector + PostgREST
npm run demo:seed    # schema + synthetic corpus
npm run demo         # cited results
npm run demo:down

请参阅 Quickstart/README.md 了解完整演练，包括启用语义（按含义匹配）演示的一次性键入步骤。

它是如何运作的

Stage 会发生什么
摄取 流解析会话记录→打开窗口→隐私预扫描→嵌入
Store Postgres + pgvector (halfvec(1024))，RLS 默认拒绝
取回 混合：pgvector cosine + tsvector lexical，通过倒数排名融合融合
重新排序 可选的交叉编码器对最佳候选者进行重新排名
回答 对源块引用了 [N] 次的综合响应

嵌入来自 Voyage（默认 voyage-4, 1024-dim）。 检索前缀生成（Contextual Retrieval）可以使用 Anthropic 或 DeepSeek。 所有提供商都使用您的 API 密钥；参见 SECURITY.md。

它索引什么

  • Claude 代码会话 — 标准 ~/.claude/projects 树（默认源）。
  • 可选导出 — Claude.ai、ChatGPT 和 Telegram 对话导出，以及通用 Markdown“内存”目录。 在 src/sources.config.ts 中配置这些（复制 src/sources.config.ts.example）。

自带语料库

  1. 为 Postgres 提供 pgvector ≥ 0.7（Supabase 项目开箱即用，或任何 PostgREST + pgvector）。
  2. 应用 supabase/migrations/ 中的迁移。
  3. 复制 .env.example → .env 并填写您的数据库 + Voyage 凭据。
  4. （可选）复制 src/sources.config.ts.example → src/sources.config.ts 以指向您自己的语料库。
  5. 摄取： npm run bootstrap:dry # 计数 + 成本估算，无写入 npm run bootstrap # 实时摄取

然后从 CLI 进行搜索（cb 搜索/cb 应答）或将 MCP 服务器连接到编辑器。

配置

所有者、品牌或型号特定的所有内容都位于 src/config.ts 中，具有中性默认值，可通过环境变量覆盖：

Env 默认 目的
CB_PRODUCT_NAME 骨髓 在横幅/提示中显示名称
CB_CLI_NAME cb 帮助文本中的 CLI 名称
嵌入模型 航程4 嵌入模型
重新排序模型 重新排序-2.5-lite 重新排序模型
默认所有者 owner 所有者键范围行

密钥（数据库、Voyage 等）来自您的环境 - 请参阅 .env.example。

故意省略的内容

这就是引擎：摄取、检索、引用。 它不会对您进行概要分析、构建推荐或在您的语料库之上进行分层分析。 这些超出了设计范围。 引擎的工作是忠实的，引用回忆 - 仅此而已。

发展

npm test          # node:test suite
npm run typecheck # tsc --noEmit
